name: az.summary
description: Create a summary table with mean, sd, credible interval, ESS, and r_hat
gotchas: ArviZ 1.0: accepts DataTree directly — no need to extract posterior
filter_vars='like' allows partial name matching
Default ci_prob=0.89 (was 0.94) and ci_kind='eti' (was 'hdi') — columns are eti_5.5%/eti_94.5%
hdi_prob= kwarg was removed — use ci_prob + ci_kind='hdi' instead
Also available as DataTree/Dataset accessor: dt.azstats.summary(...)

name: az.diagnose
description: Run a consolidated set of convergence/sampling diagnostics on a DataTree
gotchas: ArviZ 1.0 convenience entry point — checks r_hat, ESS, MCSE, divergences at once
Also available as dt.azstats.diagnose(...)

name: az.ci_in_rope
description: Fraction of the credible interval that falls inside a region of practical equivalence
gotchas: Replaces the old plot_posterior(rope=...) workflow for summary-style output
rope is a tuple (lower, upper) or dict mapping var_name -> tuple

name: az.mean
description: Posterior mean across chain/draw dims (ArviZ 1.0 point-estimate helper)
gotchas: Also as dt.azstats.mean(...)

name: az.median
description: Posterior median across chain/draw dims
gotchas: Also as dt.azstats.median(...)

name: az.mode
description: Posterior mode (for discrete variables, or via KDE for continuous)
gotchas: Also as dt.azstats.mode(...)

name: az.std
description: Posterior standard deviation
gotchas: Also as dt.azstats.std(...)

name: az.var
description: Posterior variance
gotchas: Also as dt.azstats.var(...)

name: az.iqr
description: Interquartile range of the posterior
gotchas: Also as dt.azstats.iqr(...)

name: az.mad
description: Median absolute deviation of the posterior
gotchas: Robust scale estimate; also as dt.azstats.mad(...)

name: az.hdi
description: Compute the highest density interval (HDI)
gotchas: ArviZ 1.0: hdi_prob= renamed to ci_prob=
Also as dt.azstats.hdi(...)
Use az.eti for equal-tailed intervals

name: az.eti
description: Equal-tailed credible interval (default in ArviZ 1.0 summary)
gotchas: ArviZ 1.0 default ci_kind — symmetric quantile interval
Also as dt.azstats.eti(...)

name: az.ess
description: Effective sample size (bulk, tail, or per-quantile)
gotchas: method='bulk' (default), 'tail', 'quantile', or 'local'
Check bulk ESS > 400 per chain as a rough minimum
Also as dt.azstats.ess(...)

name: az.rhat
description: Potential scale reduction factor (r_hat) convergence diagnostic
gotchas: r_hat < 1.01 indicates acceptable convergence
method='rank' is the rank-normalized split-R-hat (default)
Also as dt.azstats.rhat(...)

name: az.rhat_nested
description: Nested R-hat for designs with grouped chains (e.g. multi-start ensembles)
gotchas: Use when chains are organized in nested groups rather than exchangeable

name: az.mcse
description: Monte Carlo standard error of posterior estimates
gotchas: method='mean', 'sd', or 'quantile'
Also as dt.azstats.mcse(...)

name: az.bfmi
description: Bayesian fraction of missing information — HMC energy diagnostic
gotchas: BFMI < 0.3 indicates poor energy exploration
Requires sample_stats/energy; also as dt.azstats.bfmi(...)

name: az.kde
description: Kernel density estimate of marginal posterior(s)
gotchas: Also as dt.azstats.kde(...)

name: az.ecdf
description: Empirical CDF of samples
gotchas: Also as dt.azstats.ecdf(...)

name: az.histogram
description: Histogram of posterior samples
gotchas: Also as dt.azstats.histogram(...)

name: az.qds
description: Quantile-dot summaries (quantile dotplot support data)
gotchas: Produces input for dotplot-style visualizations

name: az.thin
description: Thin posterior samples by a factor or automatically
gotchas: Useful before expensive downstream ops; also as dt.azstats.thin(...)

name: az.weight_predictions
description: Combine posterior predictive samples from multiple models using stacking/Pseudo-BMA weights
gotchas: Pair with az.compare weights for averaged predictions
Replaces manual weighted averaging

name: az.psense
description: Prior/likelihood sensitivity via power-scaling
gotchas: Detects prior–data conflict and likelihood-driven parameters
Use psense_summary for tabular output

name: az.psense_summary
description: Tabular summary of power-scaling sensitivity diagnostics
gotchas: Flags parameters whose posterior shifts strongly under prior/likelihood perturbation

name: az.bayes_factor
description: Savage–Dickey Bayes factor for a point null hypothesis
gotchas: Requires matched prior and posterior samples
Sensitive to prior choice — interpret cautiously

name: az.kaplan_meier
description: Kaplan–Meier survival curve estimate
gotchas: Non-parametric survival baseline; pair with generate_survival_curves

name: az.generate_survival_curves
description: Generate posterior survival curves from a fitted model
gotchas: Works with parametric survival models (e.g. Weibull) in ArviZ 1.0

name: az.loo
description: PSIS-LOO-CV — expected log pointwise predictive density
gotchas: Requires log_likelihood group — call pm.compute_log_likelihood(idata, model=model) first if using nutpie
Check k-hat values — k > 0.7 indicates unreliable estimates; use loo_moment_match or reloo
Also as dt.azstats.loo(...)

name: az.loo_i
description: Per-observation PSIS-LOO for a single index i
gotchas: Useful for debugging high k-hat observations one at a time

name: az.loo_expectations
description: LOO expectations (mean, quantile, etc.) of arbitrary posterior-predictive functions
gotchas: ArviZ 1.0 API for LOO-based predictive summaries
kind='mean'|'var'|'quantile' — use probs for quantiles

name: az.loo_influence
description: Observation influence measures via LOO
gotchas: Complements k-hat for diagnosing influential observations

name: az.loo_metrics
description: LOO-weighted predictive metrics (MAE, MSE, RMSE, etc.)
gotchas: kind='mae'|'mse'|'rmse'|'acc' — cross-validated versions of standard metrics
More honest than in-sample metrics

name: az.loo_r2
description: LOO R-squared — cross-validated proportion of variance explained
gotchas: More reliable than in-sample Bayesian R2 for model selection
Requires log_likelihood group

name: az.loo_score
description: LOO-based proper scoring rules (log, CRPS, etc.)
gotchas: Generalizes elpd to alternative scoring rules

name: az.loo_pit
description: LOO probability integral transform for calibration checks
gotchas: Uniform loo_pit values indicate well-calibrated predictions

name: az.loo_approximate_posterior
description: PSIS-LOO for approximate posteriors (VI/Laplace) via importance sampling correction
gotchas: log_p and log_q are log true / log approximate density arrays over the draws
Essential when using ADVI or Laplace approximations

name: az.loo_subsample
description: Subsampled PSIS-LOO for large N (estimates elpd from a subset)
gotchas: Use when full loo is expensive; update later with update_subsample
Returns an estimate with extra Monte Carlo variance

name: az.update_subsample
description: Extend a subsampled LOO object with additional observations
gotchas: Incrementally tightens subsampled loo estimates

name: az.loo_moment_match
description: Moment-matching correction for observations with high k-hat
gotchas: Cheaper than reloo — transforms draws rather than refitting
Requires access to unconstrained parameters and log-lik closures

name: az.reloo
description: Exact refit-LOO for observations with k-hat above a threshold
gotchas: wrapper must be an az.SamplingWrapper implementation
Expensive — only refits flagged observations

name: az.compare
description: Compare models using information criteria (LOO) with stacking weights
gotchas: compare_dict maps model names to DataTree (or InferenceData-like) objects
Returns DataFrame sorted by IC (best model first)
Check dse column — large values indicate unreliable comparison
ic='waic' is no longer supported — waic was removed in ArviZ 1.0

name: az.loo_kfold
description: K-fold cross-validation elpd via a SamplingWrapper
gotchas: Alternative to LOO when PSIS fails or for grouped data
Requires an az.SamplingWrapper that knows how to refit the model

name: az.SamplingWrapper
description: Base class to wrap a model/sampler for reloo and loo_kfold
gotchas: Subclass and implement sample / log_likelihood__i / sel_observations
Needed for any CV that requires refitting

name: az.bayesian_r2
description: Bayesian R-squared (Gelman et al. 2019) — in-sample explained variance
gotchas: Use loo_r2 for cross-validated alternative

name: az.residual_r2
description: R-squared based on residual variance decomposition
gotchas: Companion to bayesian_r2 / loo_r2

name: az.kl_divergence
description: Kullback–Leibler divergence between two sample-based distributions
gotchas: Asymmetric — KL(p||q) != KL(q||p)

name: az.wasserstein
description: Wasserstein distance between empirical distributions
gotchas: Symmetric distributional distance; complements KL

name: az.metrics
description: Predictive metrics (MAE, MSE, RMSE, accuracy) against observed data
gotchas: In-sample counterpart to loo_metrics

name: az.from_netcdf
description: Load a DataTree from a NetCDF file
gotchas: ArviZ 1.0 returns xarray.DataTree, not the removed InferenceData class
Use az.to_netcdf(dt, 'file.nc') to save

name: az.to_netcdf
description: Write a DataTree to a NetCDF file
gotchas: Companion to az.from_netcdf in the ArviZ 1.0 IO API

name: az.convert_to_datatree
description: Convert dicts/arrays/framework-specific outputs to an xarray.DataTree
gotchas: Replaces the removed az.convert_to_inference_data
PyMC 6's pm.sample already returns a DataTree — no conversion needed

name: az.from_dict
description: Build a DataTree from Python dicts of arrays
gotchas: Useful for ad-hoc results or testing; all groups optional

name: az.concat
description: Concatenate DataTrees along a dimension (typically chain or draw)
gotchas: Use to stitch together results from multiple sampling runs

name: az.extract
description: Extract samples from a DataTree group, optionally subsampling
gotchas: ArviZ 1.0: returns a Dataset with combined chain/draw when combined=True
num_samples parameter for random subsampling
Useful for post-processing and plotting

name: .azstats accessor
signature: import arviz_stats; dt.azstats.<method>(...)  # or da.azstats.X / ds.azstats.X
description: xarray accessor registering summary/rhat/ess/hdi/eti/loo/mcse/bfmi/kde/... on DataTree/Dataset/DataArray
gotchas: Requires `import arviz_stats` to register the accessor
Every az.X function has a matching dt.azstats.X method with the same arguments
Preferred style in ArviZ 1.0 for chaining into xarray workflows

name: az.plot_trace
description: Plot parameter traces and marginal posteriors
gotchas: Essential first diagnostic — check for chain mixing and stationarity
Use var_names to focus on key parameters
kind='rank_bars' or 'rank_vlines' for rank-based diagnostics

name: az.plot_ppc
description: Plot posterior (or prior) predictive checks
gotchas: Requires posterior_predictive (or prior_predictive) group in the DataTree
kind='kde' for continuous, kind='cumulative' for discrete
Use observed_rug=True to show individual observations

name: az.plot_posterior
description: Plot posterior distributions with credible interval and point estimate
gotchas: ArviZ 1.0: hdi_prob= removed — use ci_prob + ci_kind instead
ref_val adds a reference line (e.g., null hypothesis)
Use rope= for a region of practical equivalence band

name: az.plot_forest
description: Forest plot comparing parameter estimates across groups/models
gotchas: combined=True pools chains; False shows each chain separately
Useful for hierarchical model parameters
model_names parameter for comparing multiple models
ArviZ 1.0: hdi_prob= replaced by ci_prob + ci_kind

name: az.plot_pair
description: Pair plot of parameter posteriors (scatter, kde, or hexbin)
gotchas: divergences=True highlights divergent transitions in red
Essential for diagnosing funnel geometry in hierarchical models
Use kind='hexbin' for many draws to avoid overplotting

name: az.plot_rank
description: Rank plot for visual convergence diagnostic
gotchas: Uniform ranks indicate good mixing; non-uniform indicates convergence issues
More sensitive than trace plots for detecting poor mixing

name: az.plot_ess
description: Plot effective sample size across quantiles or iterations
gotchas: kind='local' shows ESS for quantile estimation
kind='quantile' shows ESS at each quantile
Low ESS in tails may indicate poor exploration

name: az.plot_energy
description: Plot energy transition distribution for HMC/NUTS diagnostics
gotchas: Large gap between marginal and transition energy indicates poor exploration
Requires sample_stats group with energy variable; pair with az.bfmi

name: az.plot_loo_pit
description: LOO probability integral transform — calibration check
gotchas: Uniform distribution indicates good calibration
Requires log_likelihood and posterior_predictive groups
Part of the ArviZ 1.0 LOO diagnostics suite

name: az.plot_khat
description: Plot Pareto k-hat values from LOO-CV
gotchas: k > 0.7 = unreliable LOO estimate for that observation
k > 1.0 = very problematic; try loo_moment_match or reloo
Pass the LOO result object directly

name: az.plot_compare
description: Plot model comparison results with error bars
gotchas: Pass the DataFrame returned by az.compare
Shows ELPD difference with standard error bars
